# ProdCycle CLI

> Multi-framework, policy-as-code compliance scanner for infrastructure and application code. Evaluate Terraform, Kubernetes, Dockerfiles, CI configs, and source code in TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Java, and Ruby against SOC 2, HIPAA, and NIST CSF, powered by OPA/Rego and Cedar engines.

## Docs

- [Full Documentation](https://docs.prodcycle.com): Complete documentation

## Quick Links

- Package: `npm install -g @prodcycle/prodcycle`
- Website: [prodcycle.com](https://prodcycle.com)
- Status: [status.prodcycle.com](https://status.prodcycle.com)

## Overview

ProdCycle CLI is a multi-framework, policy-as-code compliance scanner that evaluates infrastructure and application code against SOC 2, HIPAA, and NIST CSF. It uses OPA/Rego and Cedar policy engines.

### Usage modes

- **CLI**: `prodcycle . --framework soc2,hipaa`
- **TypeScript SDK**: `scan()`, `gate()`, `runHook()`, `runHookApi()`
- **AI Agent Hooks**: Auto-configures Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI
- **REST API**: POST /v1/compliance/hook, POST /v1/compliance/validate, GET /v1/compliance/scans

### Supported frameworks

| Framework | ID | Automated Policies |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SOC 2 | soc2 | Yes |
| HIPAA | hipaa | Yes |
| NIST CSF | nist-csf | Yes |

### Supported file types

Terraform (.tf), Kubernetes (.yaml), Docker (Dockerfile), Environment (.env),
CI configs (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI), SQL (.sql), IAM policies (.json),
Source code (.ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx, .py, .go, .java, .rb)

---

# ProdCycle CLI API Details

### CLI

Scan repos from the command line. Table, JSON, SARIF, and LLM-prompt output formats.

Programmatic `scan()`, `gate()`, and hook APIs for deep integration into your toolchain.

Auto-configure compliance hooks for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot, and Gemini.

REST endpoints for real-time scanning. Perfect for CI/CD pipelines and coding agents.

SOC 2, HIPAA, and NIST CSF with tiered policies.

OPA/Rego and Cedar engines with 3-tier confidence system for infra and app code.

```bash
npm install @prodcycle/prodcycle
```

## Quickstart

Three steps to go from zero to a compliant codebase.

```typescript
import { scan, gate } from '@prodcycle/prodcycle';

// Scan a repo
const { report, exitCode } = await scan({
  repoPath: '.',
  frameworks: ['soc2', 'hipaa'],
});

console.log('Passed:', exitCode === 0);

// Or scan code strings directly
const result = await gate({
  files: { 'main.tf': terraformCode },
  frameworks: ['soc2'],
});

if (!result.passed) {
  console.log(result.prompt); // LLM-ready fixes
}
```

## Installation

Available as an npm package. A Python package is coming soon. 
Node.js ≥ 24 for the TypeScript SDK. Python SDK (≥ 3.10) is coming soon.

```bash
npm install @prodcycle/prodcycle
```

## CLI: Scan

The default command. Walks a repository, collects infrastructure and source code artifacts, evaluates them against framework policies, and outputs a report.

```bash
# Basic scan
prodcycle ./my-project

# SOC 2 + HIPAA, JSON output
prodcycle . \
  --framework soc2,hipaa \
  --format json \
  --output report.json

# SARIF for GitHub Code Scanning
prodcycle . \
  --format sarif \
  --output results.sarif

# Only Terraform files, high+ severity
prodcycle . \
  --include "**/*.tf,**/*.tfvars" \
  --severity-threshold high

# List all available frameworks
prodcycle --list-frameworks
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| repo_path | string | Path to the repository to scan |
| --framework | string | Comma-separated framework IDs (default: soc2) |
| --format | string | Output: json \| sarif \| table \| prompt (default: table) |
| --severity-threshold | string | Minimum severity to include (default: low) |
| --fail-on | string | Severities causing non-zero exit (default: critical,high) |
| --include | string | Comma-separated glob patterns to include |
| --exclude | string | Comma-separated glob patterns to exclude |
| --output | string | Write report to file instead of stdout |
| --api-key | string | API key for validation (or PC_API_KEY env) |
| --list-frameworks | flag | List available frameworks and exit |

### CLI: Hook

Real-time post-edit scanning for AI coding agents. Two modes: local and API (sends files to hosted service).

The hook reads agent tool output from stdin, extracts the edited file path, and only triggers a scan for infrastructure/source files. Non-scannable files (images, markdown, lock files) return exit code 0 immediately.

0 = pass or non-infra file, 2 = violations found (remediation prompt written to stderr).

```bash
# Local hook (reads agent output from stdin)
npx prodcycle --hook

# With explicit file path
npx prodcycle --hook --hook-file infra/main.tf

# Multiple frameworks
npx prodcycle --hook --framework soc2,hipaa
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| --hook | flag | Run as local post-edit hook |
| --hook-api | flag | Run as API-based hook (hosted scanning) |
| --hook-file | string | Explicit file path (skips stdin parsing) |
| --api-url | string | Compliance API base URL (or PC_API_URL) |
| --api-key | string | API key (or PC_API_KEY) |
| --framework | string | Frameworks to evaluate (default: soc2) |

### CLI: Init

One-command setup that auto-configures compliance hooks for your AI coding agents. Supports 6 agents out of the box.

```bash
# Configure all agents
npx prodcycle --init

# Hosted API mode
npx prodcycle --init \
  --agent claude-code,cursor,github-copilot \
  --framework soc2,hipaa \
  --api-url https://api.example.com
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| --init | flag | Run agent setup wizard |
| --agent | string | Comma-separated agents (default: all) |
| --framework | string | Frameworks to configure (default: soc2) |
| --api-url | string | Use hosted API mode |

| Agent | Hook Type | Config |
| --- | --- | --- |
| claude-code | PostToolUse | .claude/settings.json |
| cursor | afterFileEdit | .cursor/hooks.json |
| codex | Instructions | AGENTS.md |
| opencode | Instructions | AGENTS.md |
| github-copilot | Instructions | .github/copilot-instructions.md |
| gemini-cli | Instructions | GEMINI.md |

## scan()

Full repository scan. Walks the file system, collects artifacts, runs policy engines via API, and returns a structured report with findings and exit code.

```typescript
import {
  scan,
  Severity,
  formatTable,
  formatSarif,
  formatPrompt,
} from '@prodcycle/prodcycle';

const response = await scan({
  repoPath: '/path/to/repo',
  frameworks: ['soc2', 'hipaa'],
  options: {
    severityThreshold: 'medium',
    failOn: ['critical', 'high'],
    include: ['**/*.tf', '**/*.yaml'],
    exclude: ['**/test/**'],
    config: {
      application_code_tier3: true,
    },
  },
});

// Check result
console.log('Passed:', response.exitCode === 0);
console.log('Findings:', response.findings.length);

// Format output
const table = formatTable(response.report);
const sarif = formatSarif(response.report);
const prompt = formatPrompt(response.report);
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| repoPath | string | Absolute or relative path to repository |
| frameworks | string[] | Framework IDs to evaluate against |
| options | ScanOptions | Configuration options (see below) |

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| severityThreshold | Severity | Minimum severity to include (default: LOW) |
| failOn | Severity[] | Severities causing non-zero exit (default: [CRITICAL, HIGH]) |
| include | string[] | Glob patterns to include |
| exclude | string[] | Glob patterns to exclude |
| config | Record<string, unknown> | Policy config overrides (e.g. { application_code_tier3: true }) |

## gate()

Scan code strings directly without writing files to disk. Accepts a map of file paths to contents, hits the hosted API, and cleans up. Ideal for LLM-generated code, API integrations, and test harnesses.

```typescript
import { gate } from '@prodcycle/prodcycle';

const result = await gate({
  files: {
    'main.tf': `
      resource "aws_s3_bucket" "data" {
        bucket = "my-data-bucket"
      }
    `,
    '.env': `
      DB_PASSWORD=super_secret_123
    `,
  },
  frameworks: ['soc2'],
  severityThreshold: 'medium',
});

if (!result.passed) {
  console.log(result.prompt);
}
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| files | Record<string, string> | Map of relative paths to file contents |
| frameworks | string[] | Framework IDs (default: ["soc2"]) |
| severityThreshold | Severity | Min severity (default: MEDIUM) |
| failOn | Severity[] | Failure severities (default: [CRITICAL, HIGH]) |
| config | Record<string, unknown> | Policy config overrides |

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| passed | boolean | Whether all checks passed |
| exitCode | number | 0 = pass, 1 = failures, 2 = error |
| findings | Finding[] | All findings above threshold |
| prompt | string | Pre-formatted LLM remediation prompt |
| summary | object | { total, passed, failed, bySeverity } |

## runHook()

Programmatic interface for the local post-edit hook. Reads the edited file path from stdin (agent tool output) or an explicit option, skips non-infrastructure files, and runs a scan if applicable.

Returns Promise<number>: 0 = pass/skip, 2 = violations found.

```typescript
import { runHook } from '@prodcycle/prodcycle';

const exitCode = await runHook({
  frameworks: ['soc2', 'hipaa'],
  filePath: 'infra/main.tf',
});

if (exitCode !== 0) {
  console.error('Compliance violations detected');
}
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| frameworks | string[] | Frameworks to evaluate (default: ["soc2"]) |
| filePath | string | Explicit file path (skips stdin) |
| severityThreshold | Severity | Min severity (default: MEDIUM) |
| failOn | Severity[] | Failure severities |

## runHookApi()

Hosted API hook. Collects all infrastructure files from the project directory and sends them to the compliance API for centralized scanning.

```typescript
import {
  runHookApi,
  collectInfraFiles,
} from '@prodcycle/prodcycle';

// Run API hook
const exitCode = await runHookApi({
  apiUrl: 'https://api.example.com',
  apiKey: 'pc_your_key_here',
  frameworks: ['soc2'],
});
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| frameworks | string[] | Frameworks to evaluate |
| apiUrl | string | API URL (or PC_API_URL env) |
| apiKey | string | API key (or PC_API_KEY env) |
| filePath | string | Explicit file path (skips stdin) |

## Formatters

Four built-in formatters transform a ScanReport into different output formats.

- Human-readable table
- LLM-optimized prompt
- SARIF 2.1.0 for GitHub Code Scanning
- Full structured JSON

```typescript
import fs from 'node:fs';
import {
  scan,
  formatTable,
  formatPrompt,
  formatSarif,
} from '@prodcycle/prodcycle';

const { report } = await scan({
  repoPath: '.',
  frameworks: ['soc2'],
});

// Human-readable table
console.log(formatTable(report));

// LLM-ready prompt (feed to AI agent)
const prompt = formatPrompt(report);

// SARIF for GitHub
const sarif = formatSarif(report);
fs.writeFileSync('results.sarif', JSON.stringify(sarif, null, 2));

// Structured JSON
const json = report.toJson();
```

## Agent Integration

The scanner creates a self-healing compliance loop with AI coding agents. After every file write, the agent hook scans for violations and returns structured remediation instructions that the agent automatically applies.

Hook agents (Claude Code, Cursor) run scans automatically. Instruction agents (Codex, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI) receive a prompt telling them to run the scanner manually.

```bash
# One command to set up everything
npx prodcycle --init
```

## API Reference

Accepts a map of file paths to contents. Non-scannable files (images, markdown, lock files) return passed: true immediately with zero latency cost.

Designed for incremental agent scans, not full codebase scans. Limit: 500 files, 5MB payload. For full codebase scanning, use the CLI locally.

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.prodcycle.com/v1/compliance/hook \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer pc_your_key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "files": {
      "main.tf": "resource \"aws_s3_bucket\" \"data\" {\n  bucket = \"my-bucket\"\n}",
      ".env": "DB_PASSWORD=changeme"
    },
    "frameworks": ["soc2"]
  }'
```

```json
{
  "passed": false,
  "findings": [
    {
      "control_id": "SOC2-2.1-01",
      "severity": "high",
      "confidence": "high",
      "resource_type": "s3_bucket",
      "resource_path": "main.tf",
      "resource_name": "data",
      "message": "S3 bucket 'data' has no encryption at rest configured",
      "remediation": "Add aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration with aws:kms",
      "advisory": false,
      "line": 1,
      "end_line": 3,
      "engine": "rego",
      "framework": "soc2"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Fix the following compliance violations..."
}
```

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| files | Record<string, string> | Map of file paths to contents (max 500 files) |
| frameworks | string[] | Frameworks to evaluate (default: ["soc2"]) |
| product_id | string (uuid) | Associate scan with a product |

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| passed | boolean | Whether all checks passed |
| findings | Finding[] | Violation details (includes line/end_line) |
| prompt | string | LLM-ready remediation instructions |
| scanId | string | UUID of the persisted scan record |

Same input as /hook but designed for CI pipelines. Same limits apply (500 files, 5MB). For full repo scans in CI, use the CLI directly.

```yaml
name: Compliance Scan
on: pull_request

jobs:
  compliance:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '24'

      - name: Run compliance scan
        run: npx prodcycle . \
          --framework soc2,hipaa \
          --format sarif \
          --output results.sarif

      - name: Upload SARIF results
        if: always()
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        with:
          sarif_file: results.sarif
```

### API Key Management

API keys use the `pc_` prefix (64 hex characters). Keys are scoped to a workspace and support both cookie-based JWT auth and standalone Bearer token auth.

```bash
# Create API key
curl -X POST https://api.prodcycle.com/v1/compliance/api-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "description": "CI pipeline" }'

# List keys
curl https://api.prodcycle.com/v1/compliance/api-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>"

# Revoke
curl -X DELETE https://api.prodcycle.com/v1/compliance/api-keys/key_id \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>"
```

## Frameworks Reference

Three compliance frameworks ship with the scanner and include full automated OPA/Rego + Cedar policies.

| Framework | ID | Policies |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SOC 2 | soc2 | Full |
| HIPAA | hipaa | Full |
| NIST CSF 2.0 | nist-csf | Full |

## Policy Tiers

Application code policies use a three-tier confidence system. Only Tier 1 and 2 are enabled by default. Tier 3 requires explicit opt-in.

Tier 3 findings are marked advisory with a “do NOT auto-remediate” label so AI agents don't attempt fixes that could introduce regressions.

```typescript
// Enable Tier 3 via gate()
const result = await gate({
  files: { 'service.ts': code },
  config: { application_code_tier3: true },
});

// Enable Tier 3 via scan()
const response = await scan({
  repoPath: '.',
  frameworks: ['soc2'],
  options: {
    config: { application_code_tier3: true },
  },
});
```

## Severity Levels

Four severity levels, ordered by impact. By default, only critical and high findings cause a non-zero exit code.

- `critical`
- `high`
- `medium`
- `low`

## Supported File Types

The collector walks the repository and extracts normalized resource objects from these file types. Files in `node_modules`, `.terraform`, `vendor`, and `__pycache__` are always skipped.

| Type | Extensions | Extracted |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Terraform | .tf, .tf.json | Resource blocks → typed objects |
| Kubernetes | .yaml, .yml | Manifests → kind-based types |
| Docker | Dockerfile* | Base images, ports, content |
| Environment | .env, .env.* | Key-value pairs |
| CI Configs | GH Actions, GitLab CI, etc. | Jobs, steps, environments |
| SQL | .sql | Migration content |
| IAM Policies | .json (with Statement) | Actions, effects, resources |
| Source Code | .ts .tsx .js .jsx .py .go .java .rb | Content for pattern matching |

## Environment Variables

All environment variables are optional. CLI flags take precedence when both are set.

```bash
# Example .env setup
export PC_API_URL=https://api.prodcycle.com
export PC_API_KEY=pc_a1b2c3d4e5f6...
```

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| PC_API_KEY | API key for hosted scanning (pc_… prefix) |
| PC_API_URL | Base URL for hosted compliance API |
| CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR | Project root for Claude Code hooks (fallback: cwd) |
